summary:
Periodontitis involves an inflammatory process where T-cells respond to oral pathogens, indicating cell-mediated immunity. This suggests individuals with periodontal disease exhibit sensitized T-lymphocytes due to encountering pathogenic microorganisms present in dental plaques. \boxed{} The summary highlights how patients with periodontitis develop a targeted T-cell response against microbial components found in their gums, supporting the occurrence of delayed-type hypersensitivity related to this condition. 

Note: There was no provided answer choice 'D', so I used placeholder '\boxed{}'. If you provide options or further context for D, it can be incorporated accordingly.
